featuretablefilter 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference

    Calculate network connectivity metrics for features

    analyze_feature_network.Rd

    Based on a mutual information or correlation matrix, computes network centrality measures for each feature. Features with zero connections (degree = 0) are likely spurious or random artifacts.

    analyze_feature_network(
  similarity_matrix,
  threshold = NULL,
  method = c("mi", "cor"),
  automatic_threshold_method = c("mean_sd", "percentile", "mst")
)

    Arguments

    similarity_matrix

    Symmetric matrix of pairwise similarities (mutual information or correlation).

    threshold

    Cutoff value above which an edge is considered significant. For MI, typical values are 0.1-0.5 bits. For correlation, typical values are 0.3-0.7. Default is NULL (automatic).

    method

    Type of similarity measure: "mi" for mutual information, "cor" for Pearson correlation. Used for automatic thresholding.

    automatic_threshold_method

    Method for automatic threshold selection: "mean_sd" (mean + 2*SD), "percentile" (75th percentile), or "mst" (minimum spanning tree). Default is "mean_sd".

    Value

    A list containing:

    degree_centrality

    Number of significant connections per feature

    strength

    Sum of connection strengths per feature

    betweenness

    Betweenness centrality (if igraph available)

    threshold_used

    The threshold value used

    n_edges

    Total number of edges in the network

    adjacency_matrix

    Binary adjacency matrix (1 = connected, 0 = not)

    Examples

    # Compute MI matrix first
# mi_matrix <- compute_mutual_information(feature_table)

# Get network metrics
# network_metrics <- analyze_feature_network(mi_matrix)

# Features with zero degree are likely artifacts
# disconnected_features <- names(network_metrics$degree_centrality[
#   network_metrics$degree_centrality == 0
# ])

    Contents
